rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW GEORGE JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the amendment filed on 9/12/23 has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) The proof of service filed on 9/11/23 was not of the proper Notice of Hearing, which is a mandatory Judicial Council form and to which the instructions for how to appear remotely must be attached. (The correct proof of service is on the reverse side of that form DE-120.) (3) The assets on hand at the beginning of this accounting period now differ from the assets on hand at the end of the last accounting period. The total was $92,171.31. This one starts with $91,971.84. (4) Why were items totaling $10,437.41 donated to charity (not sold)? Was Notice of Proposed Action given? (5) Itemization should show WHAT was filed, etc., not just "electronic filing service." Deadline for filing responsive papers is 11/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIA FAIL 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order After Hearing On July 17, 2023 Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling.) Before the Court is Petitioner's motion for reconsideration of the Court's July 17, 2023 Order removing Gregory Crowe as Trustee of the Lia Fail Trust and appointing Leslie Anderson-Adams as Successor Trustee. Petitioner moves for reconsideration on the grounds that (1) neither the Court nor any party had requested Crowe's removal as Trustee; and (2) Anderson-Adams's appointment is legally improper because of the various conflicts of interest involved. The motion is DENIED. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, subdivision (a), "When an application for an order has been made to a judge, or to a court, and refused in whole or in part, or granted, or granted conditionally, or on terms, any party affected by the order may, within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order and based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, make an application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order. The party making the application shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." Although timely filed, the instant motion fails to persuade. With respect to Petitioner's first argument-that neither party had requested Crowe's removal as Trustee nor proposed that Ms. Anderson-Adams be appointed as successor trustee-these are neither new nor different facts supporting reconsideration. The Court has authority to act sua sponte to make any orders and take any other action necessary in a trust litigation, including appointment of a temporary trustee to administer the trust in whole or in part. (Probate Code, Section 17206; see also Section 14652(a).) The "fact" that no one requested Petitioner's removal is not a new fact or a different fact, it is simply an irrelevant fact. Removal of Mr. Crowe as trustee was amply supported by the state of the record before the Court. Among other things, Mr. Crowe was unable to obey the Court's order that he deposit funds into a blocked account, he was unable to fully account for the funds derived from the sale of trust assets, and he repeatedly asked the Court to delay court proceedings based on his ill health. His health concerns alone justify removal as trustee-a claimed inability to participate in litigation for months and months due to ill health surely means that one is also unable to act as trustee for the same reasons. Appointing Ms. Anderson-Adams as temporary trustee, the person nominated as a potential successor trustee by the trustor in the Lia Fail Trust, was within the Court's discretion. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIA FAIL 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order After Hearing On July 17, 2023 Removing Trustee And Appointing Successor Trustee (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling.) With respect to the second argument, a motion for reconsideration is the incorrect vehicle for asserting perceived conflicts of interest in the temporary trustee. Granting a motion for reconsideration would return the parties to the status quo ante, with Mr. Crowe as trustee. As explained above, the Court rejects that outcome. If Petitioner believes that the temporary trustee cannot perform due to conflicts of interest, he should bring a noticed petition to remove and replace her. (See, Probate Code, Section 14652.) At present, the stakeholders for the competing trusts appear poised to present their competing views of which trust is operative to a finder of fact and seek a judicial resolution of their dispute. Petitioner has not demonstrated how the Court's order removing Mr. Crowe and appointing Ms. Anderson-Adams as temporary trustee will interfere with his ability to argue that the 2004 Lia Fail Trust is operative and controlling. If he believes that to be the case, a petition to remove and replace would be the appropriate vehicle for bringing that issue to the Court, which could decide the issue on full and complete briefing. For the reasons stated, the motion for reconsideration is DENIED. Counsel for temporary trustee Ms. Anderson-Adams shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (End of Tentative Ruling.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/2023. The supplement filed on 09/29/2023 incorrectly increased gains and property on hand at close of account by the difference between the estimated market value and carry value of the real property. Probate Code section 1063(a) requires a separate schedule to state the ending market value, but the market value is not part of charges or credits. The supplemented accounting schedules must be corrected.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CELIA R. JERLABAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 05/17/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged - in light of the related civil case (quiet title action), distribution must be ordered subject to the civil plaintiff (lien holder)'s interest in the real property to be determined in that action. 2) A waiver of account signed by the petitioner in his capacity as trustee of the devisee trust must be filed with his acceptance of the trust. See Probate Code section 10954(b)(4). 3) The attorney's statutory compensation and costs must be ordered to be paid out of the estate under section 10831(d). An allegation must be made as to whether the petitioner has advanced sufficient cash to the estate for the payment out of the estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAN PAL HUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/29/2023, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide the petitioner's telephone number at item 2 of the acknowledgment of receipt section. 4) Re the DE-121 form: i) Item 1 must specify all names (aka) by which the decedent was known. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. ii) Notice must given to the alternate executors named in the will. 5) Re the proof of publication: All names (aka) by which the decedent was known must be published. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. 6) Item 3d: The petitioner appears to be the sole beneficiary under the will. Please explain why the petitioner is not proceeding with a spousal property petition. Note: If the petitioner intends to make an election under Probate Code section 13502, the election must be filed within 4 months after letters are issued and must include language required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5) ("statement must clearly state that she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees.") 7) Item 3f(2): The will appears to be self-proving, but the box was not checked. 8) Item 4 must be checked. 9) Item 8 must list the alternate executors named in the will and their address and relationship to the decedent. If no satisfactory progress is shown to address those issues, an appearance may be required on 11/29/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar.
